meta.pageTitle: Timothy J. Owen — Data Center Technician Resume
meta.eyebrow: Data Center Technician | Oracle WaV2T Pipeline

header.name: Dr. Timothy J. Owen
header.subtitle: U.S. Army Veteran · Data Center Technician · South Puget Sound, WA

objective: Washington Vets-to-Tech graduate and retired 20-year U.S. Army veteran with hands-on training in server administration, cloud infrastructure, networking, and cybersecurity. Proven record of maintaining mission-critical operations around the clock, executing structured procedures with precision, and documenting complex processes for accountability and continuity. Holds an active **federal Secret-level security clearance**. Holding **CompTIA Network+**, **AWS Cloud Practitioner**, **Foundational C#**, and **PCEP Python** certifications. Ready to apply military-forged operational discipline and WaV2T technical training to Oracle's data center environment.

transferableSkills.title: Operational Discipline
transferableSkills.description: 20 years maintaining 100% mission readiness across 400+ high-stakes operations — the same standard Oracle's 24/7 data centers require every shift.

transferableSkills.title: Documentation Rigor
transferableSkills.description: Maintained training records for 80 personnel, assessed 400+ performances, and contributed to policy development affecting 5,000+ employees — directly applicable to data center runbooks and escalation logs.

transferableSkills.title: Automation & Scripting
transferableSkills.description: Built Python tools for subnet calculations, VA ratings, and tempo optimization — applying algorithmic problem-solving to real infrastructure and support workflows.

transferableSkills.title: Security & Compliance
transferableSkills.description: Administered federal Secret-level clearance compliance for 40+ personnel and completed two WaV2T security programs — bringing both operational and academic security discipline to data center work.

bullets: Executed IT service requests in a regulated government environment, maintaining procedural compliance and detailed documentation — directly parallel to data center ticketing and escalation workflows
Processed constituent records with 100% compliance with state data privacy policies across a 3-month engagement
Communicated technical information clearly to non-technical stakeholders
Completed 16-week intensive program earning 18 college credits toward a B.S. in Computer Science — 100% lab completion rate
Mastered information security, ethical hacking, and threat intelligence (CSC 325, CSC 364, CSC 426, CSC 455)
Implemented multi-factor authentication and Transport Layer Security encryption; ensured HIPAA compliance in simulated clinical environments
Advanced expertise in network server architectures and cloud computing, configuring Windows Server and deploying AWS applications
Completed 16-week intensive program earning 18 college credits toward a B.S. in Computer Science — 100% lab completion rate
Configured network environments (IPv4/v6, subnetting, VPN); administered Linux and Windows Server via PowerShell
Deployed AWS EC2 applications and configured Ansible for infrastructure automation
Developed Python automation scripts for end-user support tasks and data analysis
Sustained mission-critical operations across 400+ high-stakes performances with 100% readiness — the round-the-clock operational standard data centers demand
Managed $300K in equipment inventory with zero discrepancy, applying asset accountability and hardware lifecycle discipline central to data center technician work
Administered federal security clearance compliance for 40+ personnel with precision and confidentiality

Facilitated training and certification of 200 Master Resilience Trainers as a Level 2 Facilitator — demonstrating the structured instruction and documentation discipline that data center runbooks require
Instructed 100+ new trainees and 20 mid-level leaders — 100% program pass rate; maintained training records for 80 personnel
Advised 40 senior leaders as subject-matter expert in administering field assessments, improving assessment quality and consistency
Assessed 400+ student performances; coached 12 small and medium-sized teams
Contributed to curriculum and policy development for 5,000+ personnel, resulting in 58% reduction in training cycle time
Earned Distinguished Leader Award, Army Leadership Development Program (2010)
Maintained operational readiness across 120–150 performances including 60 international assignments; deployed in support of Operation Iraqi Freedom (2007)
Managed HR administration and library assets for 40+ personnel with full documentation accuracy
Earned promotion to Sergeant (E-5) in 2008
